study_group_representative.quote: As of 31 March 2020, a total of 9786 confirmed cases with COVID-19 have been reported in South Korea. ... The Korea Centres for Disease Control and Prevention (KCDC) reported the basic epidemiological characteristics of 7755 patients with COVID-19 in South Korea as of 13 March 2020 using surveillance data retrieved from the KCDC-operated National Notifiable Disease Surveillance System.

intervention_and_participants_defined.quote: As of 31 March 2020, a total of 9786 confirmed cases with COVID-19 have been reported in South Korea.

outcome_assessed_for_60pct.quote: The Korea Centres for Disease Control and Prevention (KCDC) reported the basic epidemiological characteristics of 7755 patients with COVID-19 in South Korea as of 13 March 2020 using surveillance data retrieved from the KCDC-operated National Notifiable Disease Surveillance System.

follow_up_length_reported.quote: The median interval between onset of symptom and death was 10 days (range 1–24 days), while the median interval between date of hospitalisation and the date of death was 5 days (range 0–16 days).

outcome_assessors_blinded.quote: Pan coronavirus conventional PCR assay was positive for the throat swab sample, and sequencing of the PCR amplicon showed that the sequence was identical to that of the 2019-nCoV isolated from the Wuhan patient.

outcome_definition_objective_precise.quote: The case fatality proportion was 0.1% among the age groups of 30–39 and 40–49 years, then increased to 0.4% (50–59 years), 1.5% (60–69 years), 5.0% (70–79 years) and 8.5% (≥80 years).

important_prognostic_factors_accounted_f.quote: The female-to-male ratio was 62:38. The age group of 20–29 years accounted for 28.9% of all cases, followed by the age groups of 50–59 and 40–49 years.

analysis_described_and_effect_quantified.quote: This report aimed to describe the current situation of COVID-19 in South Korea and our response to this outbreak.
